stimulus: As biologist Susan Williams has documented, long-distance migration presents a challenge for hummingbirds and other small migrating birds. A hummingbird must store enough fat to fuel itself across hundreds of miles of open water, while keeping its body weight low enough that it can still maintain its rapid wingbeat. ______blank a hummingbird feeds almost constantly in the weeks before migration, building up fat reserves to nearly double its normal body weight.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "For this reason" logically signals that the behavior described in this sentence is a consequence of the information about hummingbirds in the previous sentence. That is, a hummingbird feeds almost constantly before migration because it needs to store enough fat to fuel itself without becoming too heavy.

Choice A is incorrect because "in the first place" illogically signals the first point in a discussion. Instead, the sentence describes a behavior that is a consequence of the previous information. Choice B is incorrect because "on the other hand" illogically signals contrast. Instead, the behavior is a consequence of the previous information. Choice D is incorrect because "in comparison" illogically signals a comparison. Instead, the behavior is a consequence of the previous information.
